articles.title: No one had an issue until I pointed my lens at Palestine | Misan Harriman
articles.summary: No one had an issue until I pointed my lens at Palestine | Misan Harriman Submitted by Mohamed Hashem on Mon, 07/20/2026 - 11:08 "I am now getting death threats from registered Telegraph readers." Misan Harriman is a widely celebrated photographer and activist. He’s the first Black male photographer to shoot the cover of British Vogue and the director of an Oscar-nominated short film. In this conversation on Real Talk, he speaks to Mohamed Hashem about his work, activism, and what happened when he turned his lens to Palestine. articles.title: Israeli physician describes Israeli court hearing over humanitarian corridor as 'fascist'
articles.summary: Israeli physician describes Israeli court hearing over humanitarian corridor as 'fascist' Michal Feldon, a paediatric rheumatologist at Yitzhak Shamir Medical Centre, described a hearing at the High Court of Justice regarding the opening of the humanitarian corridor following a petition filed by doctors as "another farce of a fascist regime". In a petition filed by Physicians for Human Rights, the organisation asked for the humanitarian corridor to be opened, to allow the transfer of critically ill patients from Gaza to the West Bank and occupied East Jerusalem for treatment. articles.summary: US attacks Iran's southern city of Bushehr US strikes several locations in Iran's southern port city of Bushehr, home to the country's civilian nuclear plant, state news media reported.
